name: North Dakota SNAP
description: North Dakota's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to low-income households to purchase groceries. Administered by the North Dakota Department of Health and Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: North Dakota Medicaid
description: North Dakota's Medicaid program providing health coverage including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, dental, vision, and behavioral health services for eligible residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults 19-64: up to 138% FPL. Children: up to 175% FPL.

name: North Dakota Healthy Steps (CHIP)
description: North Dakota's Children's Health Insurance Program providing low-cost health coverage for uninsured children whose families earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income above Medicaid limits
incomeLimitNotes: Family income between 175% and 200% of federal poverty level

name: North Dakota Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to North Dakota workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by Job Service North Dakota.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements

name: North Dakota Housing Finance Agency (NDHFA)
description: North Dakota's housing finance agency providing affordable housing programs including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, down payment assistance, and homelessness prevention.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income North Dakota residents seeking housing assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 80% area median income

name: Legal Services of North Dakota
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income North Dakotans in areas including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and Native American law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income North Dakota residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Job Service North Dakota
description: Provides workforce development services through local offices including job search assistance, career counseling, skills training, labor market information, and employer connections.
eligibilitySummary: All North Dakota residents seeking employment or career development services

name: North Dakota LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Helps low-income North Dakota households pay heating and cooling costs. Provides seasonal heating assistance and emergency energy crisis intervention during harsh winters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income North Dakota households with heating costs
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 60% of state median income

name: North Dakota 211 FirstLink
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting North Dakotans to local health and human services including food, housing, utilities, healthcare, and crisis intervention.
eligibilitySummary: All North Dakota residents seeking community resource information

name: Great Plains Food Bank
description: North Dakota's Feeding America affiliate food bank serving all counties in North Dakota and Clay County, Minnesota. Distributes millions of pounds of food through 200+ partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Any North Dakota resident experiencing food insecurity

name: North Dakota Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Provides services to North Dakota veterans including benefits claims assistance, county veterans service officers, education benefits, and connections to VA healthcare.
eligibilitySummary: North Dakota veterans and their eligible dependents

name: North Dakota Crisis Line (988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
description: Statewide crisis hotline providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress. Operated by FirstLink in partnership with 988 Lifeline.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in North Dakota experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: North Dakota Child Care Assistance
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Working families with children under 13 meeting income and employment requirements
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 85% of state median income

name: North Dakota TANF
description: Cash assistance program for low-income North Dakota families with dependent children, providing monthly benefits along with work-related services and supportive services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children meeting work participation requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size

name: North Dakota WIC
description: Provides nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy food benefits, and healthcare referrals for low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: North Dakota Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income North Dakota households including insulation, air sealing, and heating system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: North Dakota Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment services to North Dakotans with disabilities including career counseling, skills training, assistive technology, and job placement to help find and maintain employment.
eligibilitySummary: North Dakota residents with disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: North Dakota Aging Services
description: Provides services for North Dakota seniors including Meals on Wheels, senior centers, in-home care, caregiver support, and the Senior Health Insurance Counseling (SHIC) program.
eligibilitySummary: North Dakota residents age 60 and older, and their caregivers

name: North Dakota Domestic Violence Crisis Line
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking in North Dakota

name: MATBUS (Fargo-Moorhead Area Transit)
description: Public transit service for the Fargo-Moorhead metropolitan area providing fixed-route bus service and paratransit. Reduced fares for seniors and persons with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: All Fargo-Moorhead area residents; reduced fares for seniors and persons with disabilities

name: Fargo-Moorhead Emergency Food Pantry
description: Provides free groceries to individuals and families in need in the Fargo-Moorhead area, distributing food through client-choice shopping and partner programs.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of the Fargo-Moorhead area experiencing food insecurity

name: City of Fargo Housing and Community Development
description: Administers federal housing programs in Fargo including CDBG, HOME, Housing Choice Vouchers, and homelessness prevention programs for low-income residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-to-moderate income residents of Fargo
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 80% area median income

name: North Dakota Protection and Advocacy Project
description: The state's designated protection and advocacy agency providing free legal services to people with disabilities on issues including access to services and civil rights.
eligibilitySummary: North Dakota residents with disabilities facing rights violations or needing legal advocacy

name: North Dakota Head Start
description: Comprehensive early childhood education for low-income North Dakota families providing education, health screenings, nutrition, and family support for children birth to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children birth to age 5 and pregnant women
incomeLimitNotes: At or below 100% of federal poverty level; categorical eligibility for certain groups

name: North Dakota Adult Education
description: Provides free adult basic education, GED preparation, ESL classes, and workforce readiness programs for North Dakota adults seeking to improve education and employment skills.
eligibilitySummary: North Dakota adults age 16 and older not enrolled in school

name: Bismarck-Mandan Transit (CAT)
description: Public transit service for the Bismarck-Mandan metropolitan area providing fixed-route and demand-response bus service for all residents.
eligibilitySummary: All Bismarck-Mandan area residents

name: North Dakota New Americans Program
description: Provides support services for refugees and immigrants in North Dakota including employment assistance, English classes, cultural orientation, and connections to community resources.
eligibilitySummary: Refugees and immigrants newly arrived in North Dakota
languages: English, and multiple languages through interpreters

name: Community Action Partnership - Minot Region
description: Provides community action services to north central North Dakota including energy assistance, weatherization, Head Start, transportation, and emergency services for low-income families.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of the Minot region of North Dakota

name: North Dakota Rural Transit Network
description: Statewide network of rural and small urban transit providers offering affordable transportation throughout North Dakota, particularly for medical appointments and essential errands.
eligibilitySummary: All North Dakota residents, with emphasis on rural communities, seniors, and persons with disabilities
